message:
🐛 Fix kind E2E cluster creation: use kind get clusters + export kubeconfig

The kc-agent creates clusters asynchronously. The previous test used
kubectl cluster-info to check readiness, but the kubeconfig context
wasn't always exported yet. Fix:
- Use `kind get clusters` to detect creation (doesn't need kubeconfig)
- Run `kind export kubeconfig` explicitly after creation
- Use `kubectl get nodes` for API server reachability check
- Add buffer time to provisioning timeout

Tested: 3/8 passing (deploys + cleanup work, verification timing
needs more tuning for pod readiness waits).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
